entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Adaptation Transport Organizer
entry.content:
Thank you very much for exaplaining @Jocelyn_Dart .

As the custom fields need a workbench request, am I right in assumining that, for the custom fields/workbench req we either need to have a Fiori enviromant connected to our customising client with working data or open up unit testing client to modification and create the transport there and move to the actual configuration client?

Thanks and Regards,

Sathya

entry.title: Re: modifying the defaut email address in s4 output param...
entry.content: In fact, Surya, currently my system takes the "standard communication email adress" as default email adress when emailing Billing Documents. And I would like to change this behaviour so that the system takes the "Clerk Internet email adress" maintained in the BP data. Do you think it is possible ?

entry.title: Re: How to send Email Notifications only on the Last Retry Attempt in SAP CI/IS when using AEM
entry.content: Nicely explained !!

entry.title: Re: Another chapter of Secure By Default for SAP S/4HANA 2023
entry.content:
Hi again,

I had meanwhile the chance to check it on my own on a fresh HANA installation. It looks like none of the assumed options apply. During installation, there is an Active Access Rule created for every Rule Definition with the same status. But the status can still be something <> Active.

Thanks,
Thomas

entry.title: Re: RECADV not available in WE81
entry.content: I need to send to the vendor the receiving confirmation

entry.title: MDC Customer Add Custom Table Missing Data on Entity
entry.content:
Hello, I have implemented the procedure to extend the massive consolidation process on the BP customer with a custom table by following this guide:

https://www.sap.com/documents/2017/08/18cfbb67-cd7c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa.html

I have re-implemented the methods of the two classes CL_MDC_DATA_BP and CL_MDC_MODEL_BP, and by running tests, I have verified that the data is correctly written into the API structures within the method MAP_EXTENSIONS_2API. However, how can I write data into the entity before the customer is activated? The activation process runs validations that generate errors and prevent activation because the data are missing in my custom entuty, Maybe a mapping is missing, a method needs to be redefined, or a BAdI is required to write the data into the correct entity.

Can anyone help me solve this issue?

Thanks in advance.

Antonio

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model: Parameter Usage
entry.content:
Hello! If you've read one of our previous blogpost about Complex Selection in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Universal Model (SAP PaPM Cloud UM), you might've seen that users can declare a created Parameter in Complex Selection to further filter relevant data.

In SAP PaPM Cloud UM, users build their environments according to business requirements and scenarios. To process data efficiently, users can utilize Parameters which can be considered during data processing.

As defined in our Application Help for SAP PaPM Cloud UM:

Parameters are used to steer processes and calculations. They are used in formulas and calls of certain functions and influence the applied logic and operations.

In this blogpost, we will discuss Parameter usage in SAP PaPM Cloud UM with the following sample scenarios:

  1. Declare Parameter in Complex Selection of a Join
  2. Declare Parameter in Formula of a Calculation

Then for each scenario, we will be defining a Parameter value in the Activity so it can be triggered in a Process. Let's get started!

Note: This blogpost contains images. In case the images are too small, kindly click on the image to view with a better resolution.

Scenario 1: Declare Parameter in Complex Selection of a Join

For this scenario, the Parameter CTypeParam (Customer Type Parameter) has been created in the Environment.

We will also be using the following input Model Entities which will be combined by a Join function Type: Inner Join.

In the Join function, the Parameter we created will be used and declared to perform Complex Selection within its Complex Predicates editor. To declare a parameter in SAP PaPM Cloud UM, the following the format is used:

 

SESSION_CONTEXT('<parameter_fieldid>')

 

The <parameter_fieldid> specifies the Field ID of the Parameter which will be used by the syntax in our Complex Selection.

Syntax used:

 

CustomerProfileTable.Customer = CustomerDepositTable.Customer
WHERE CustomerProfileTable.CustomerType = SESSION_CONTEXT('CTypeParam')

 

To further understand the syntax used, we declared our predicate using the function ID of the first Input CustomerProfileTable with its field Customer which is also existing in the second Input CustomerDepositTable.

Then, we have declared a Complex Selection that will search for entries wherein the field CustomerType is equal to the value of CTypeParam that will be defined in the Activity Parameter.

Now that we've declared the Parameter in the function, let’s proceed with defining the Parameter value under the Activity Parameters of the Activity that is mapped to the Join function. To do so, kindly follow the steps:

  1. Create an Activity within a Process.
    Note: Activity Function of an Activity should have its API State: Active.
  2. Under Activity Parameters, choose the created Parameter field that was declared in the function.
  3. Define the Parameter value in Formula.
  4. Choose Save.
  5. Choose Run button in the Activity.
  6. Once Run is finished, choose Show Data.

The result of the Activity will display the data of the processed Join function which were filtered based on the defined conditional parameter. Since we declared in the Complex Selection of Join that the field Customer Type should be equal to the value of Customer Type Parameter (CTypeParam) that was defined as REGULAR in the Formula of the Activity Parameter, the result data only displayed entries whose Customer Type is REGULAR.

Scenario 2: Declare Parameter in Formula of a Calculation

Aside from Join, we can also declare Parameters in the Formula of a Calculation function.

For example, we would like to calculate the interest of the Deposit Amount from the result data of the Join function from our previous scenario. Having this, the Parameter field InterestParam has been created.

In the Calculation function, this InterestParam parameter has been declared in the Formula of newly created field Deposit with Interest (DepositWithInterest) using below syntax:

 

= DepositAmount + (DepositAmount * SESSION_CONTEXT('InterestParam'))

 

Here, we are declaring that the value of InterestParam (which will be defined in the Activity Parameter) will be multiplied with the value of the DepositAmount. The product of both values would then be added to the value of DepositAmount to determine the total value of our field Deposit with Interest (DepositWithInterest).

Since the parameter InterestParam has been declared in the function, we can already proceed with defining the Parameter value under the Activity Parameters of the Activity that is mapped to the Calculation function – just like what we did in the first scenario. In this example, we will define its value as 0.005.

Once the Activity is Run, the result of the Activity will display the data of the processed Calculation function that was influenced by the defined formula and its parameter value.

In addition, Parameters can be of different types such as Characteristic or Key Figure which can be used depending on your requirement.

That's it! I hope you found this blogpost about usage of SESSION_CONTEXT for Parameter declaration helpful with modeling in SAP PaPM Cloud UM. You may find more blogposts related to SAP PaPM Cloud UM with the following tag: PaPMCloudUM.

See you again on the next one!

entry.title: Delivery address is not being referenced from ship to party of sales order in third party scenario
entry.content:
In a third-party scenario, if I create a PR from a Sales Order, the address has to be copied from the ship-to-party address to the Delivery address tab of the PR. The same delivery address should be copied from the PR to the respective PO. This should work even if I update the ship-to-party address in my sales order. This works as expected for the Customized tenant.

 

In the Production system, when a user creates a sales order for a third-party scenario and updates the ship-to address, the related PR does not copy this address and shows no ref address number. Hence, the PO also displays the old ship-to address from the Sales order, which is wrong.

 

The apps Manage Purchase Requisition and Manage Purchase Requisition- Professional, both show different address numbers.

 

As the system determines the ship to address, please help to fix this.

==============================================================================

 

Dear Customer,

https://me.sap.com/notes/498153

Kindly  check the 4th question in the FAQ

 

Thanks and Regards

SAP SUPPORT

entry.title: Re: RFC Connection Error
entry.content:
Hi,

in case you are using for the RFC connection logongroups , then you need to do a couple of additional steps:

1) Set up VPN from CCv2 to customer network, add remote systems and NAT rules for all required message servers and (!) application servers.

2) Define a new logon group in the SAP system, add the required application servers and maintain the NAT IPs on logon group / application server assignment. (optional)

3) Add host aliases for the message servers and configure the message server connection in the RFC Destination configuration in Commerce Backoffice. Use the logon group defined in step 2.

4) For every application server, add the NAT IP to parameter gw/alternative_hostnames in the instance profile.

Some explanation:
If instance profile parameter gw/alternative_hostnames was not maintained, the gateway process does not recognize that the connection should go to itself, so it tries to forward it to the NAT IP which wouldn't work in the customer network. With gw/alternative_hostnames maintained, it recognizes that the IP belongs to itself and establishes to connection with the application server.

KBA for more details: https://me.sap.com/notes/1760329

Best Regards,
Kai

entry.title: Re: Delivery address is not being referenced from ship to...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

https://me.sap.com/notes/498153

Kindly  check the 4th question in the FAQ

 

Thanks and Regards

SAP SUPPORT

entry.title: Re: How the proposed PIR is calculated in Maintain PIR
entry.content:
Hi,

I think it takes average of sales qty from the first month that has sales qty to last month.

In your case, Proposed PIRs for M12.2024 = (Sales qty of M7.2024 + M8 + M9 + M10 + M11)/ 5 = 26/5 = 5 PC.

I tested same result.

Proposed PIR for M2.2025 = (50 + 0 + 200 + 300 + 0) / 5 = 110 PC

entry.title: Re: Doubt for optimize abap code in BADI
entry.content: Although SELECT FOR ALL ENTRIES should be avoided in modern ABAP, it's a good alternative for newbies because it's simple and improves the performance a little bit. Did you try it?

entry.title: ByD Destination Issue Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi all!

I'm new in the BTP developement and am facing some issues. Specifically in setting up the connection between the BTP and my company's tenant in ByD.

I've looked already into all previous issues and tutorials, but neither helped me solving my problem. 

I've already set up a destination in my btp cockpit twice: One for my actions (not specific, tests as "302 redirect") and one for my Apps (specific, tests as "200 OK", that points to an oData Service as mentioned here  My first steps with BTP destinations (S/4HANA) in ... - SAP Community    ) 

 

When adding the Actions to my Build env, i can see the data in the playground, when i try to execute the action in my test App either i don't see the request beeing made at all or i see an Error 500. 

When I try to add the destination as an Integration i get Error 404 for the one i used for my Actions and 400 for the one i made specific for the App. Bottomline I can't add them in this way. 

destination details:

{
  "name": "byd_test_integration_CLONING",
  "description": "a connection to the test environment",
  "mobile_enabled": false,
  "action_enabled": true,
  "apiType": "catalog",
  "sapSalesAndServiceCloud": {
    "is_mashup": false,
    "is_api_metadata": false,
    "is_api": false
  },
  "is_catalog": true,
  "is_appgyver_metering": false,
  "is_dynamic": true
}

request details:

{
  "headers": {
    "Accept": "application/json, text/plain, */*",
    "Content-Type": "application/json",
    "x-csrf-token": "xxx",
    "x-requested-with": "XMLHttpRequest"
  },
  "baseURL": "/destinations_api/v2/destination/byd_test_integration_CLONING",
  "data": "{\"headers\":{\"accept\":\"application/json, text/plain, */*\"},\"url\":\"\",\"method\":\"get\"}",
  "adapter": [
    "xhr",
    "http",
    "fetch"
  ]
}

respone details:

{
  "status": 400,
  "headers": {
    "content-length": "188",
    "content-type": "application/json; charset=utf-8",
    "date": "Thu, 09 Jan 2025 09:13:17 GMT",
    "etag": "W/\"bc-tSIwzw3CuFMtYZmKug+emEGrR1U\"",
    "strict-transport-security": "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload",
    "x-correlation-id": "xxx",
    "x-frame-options": "DENY",
    "x-request-id": "xxx"
  },
  "data": {
    "error": "Error: Request failed with status code 400\n---\nRequest failed with status code 400\n",
    "message": "Request failed with status code 400\n---\nRequest failed with status code 400"
  }
}

Instance details:

{
  "tenant_id": "xxx",
  "user_id_for_sentry": "JWTUserId:xxx",
  "environment": "eu10",
  "location": "https://xxx/builder/applications/176610/data",
  "cfdomain": "xxx",
  "enabled_features": [
    "newTopNav",
    "releaseManagement",
    "adobeTracking",
    "getUserInfo",
    "vcfEnableOpaAuthorization",
    "actionIntegration",
    "useMobileAppRepackaging",
    "useWebBuildDependencyProxy",
    "customRequestHeader"
  ],
  "datetime": "2025-01-09T09:13:18.808Z"
}

When I try to integrate as legacy entities, both destinations aren't discovered in the SAP BTP Destination REST API Integration as options. 

I tryied adding the Endpoint  as Universal REST Api Integration, but I get 401 Unauthorized as an answer even though i've explicitely set the Authorization header with the correct base64 credential. 

I've also tryied adding as plain oData, but again, 401 not Authorized. 

Is there a standard way to add a ByD Destination with which i can interact in a Build Apps use case? Or a step by step guide? I really doubt that I'm the first one facing such problem...

entry.title: Re: UI5 Loading json file
entry.content: can you please tell me how to make it async to sync

entry.title: Re: Hybrid Partner Add-ons for on-premise & BTP ABAP SaaS
entry.content:
@EC we typcially use SAP Cals to verify we can create a proof of concepts in Z space then get ride of the system after the trial. We create and license our own permenant systems for partner landscapes.

But since writing the blog, I am concerned that even the newest available on-premise system (2023 FSP02) does not contain 'Unified Connectivity Tooling'. One result is we don't have the ability set-up communcation arrangements or communication targets in on-premise systems. So.. We may eventually still end up with a 4 system BTP ABAP environment landscape. 

__PRESENT

__PRESENT

__PRESENT

entry.title: Working Capital Management in SAP S/4HANA: Innovations and Benefits
entry.content:
In today’s dynamic economic environment, organizations face increased challenges in managing liquidity and optimizing working capital. Corporate treasury has become a cornerstone for navigating these turbulent times, integrating financial risk into decision-making processes, and ensuring business continuity. SAP has consistently invested in its Treasury and Working Capital Management (WCM) solutions to help organizations adapt and thrive. With the release of SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud in 2022, key innovations have further strengthened the tools available for effective WCM.

Why Working Capital Management Matters Today

Working capital—the difference between current assets and current liabilities—is a critical indicator of an organization’s financial health. Effective WCM ensures sufficient liquidity to meet short-term obligations while optimizing the use of available resources. In challenging economic climates, organizations need robust systems to:

  • Enhance liquidity management: Ensuring there is enough cash flow to support day-to-day operations.
  • Optimize financial supply chains: Managing payables and receivables efficiently to reduce costs.
  • Mitigate risks: Identifying and addressing financial risks that could impact operations.

SAP’s Portfolio for Working Capital Management

SAP’s portfolio for WCM is designed to deliver end-to-end solutions that integrate seamlessly into an organization’s financial ecosystem. The 2022 SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud releases include several enhancements, including:

1. Integration with the Taulia Platform

Taulia, a leading platform for working capital optimization, now complements SAP’s WCM solutions by enabling:

  • Supply Chain Financing: Facilitating early payment programs that benefit both suppliers and buyers.
  • Dynamic Discounting: Allowing organizations to negotiate flexible payment terms based on liquidity needs.
  • Enhanced Cash Flow Visibility: Providing real-time insights into cash flow to inform better decision-making.

2. Enhanced Liquidity Management

New features in SAP S/4HANA’s Liquidity Management module enable:

  • Improved cash flow forecasting through machine learning algorithms.
  • Comprehensive visibility across global accounts to streamline decision-making.
  • Automated reconciliation processes that save time and reduce errors.

3. Advanced Analytics for Treasury

Organizations now have access to robust analytics tools to:

  • Monitor working capital metrics in real time.
  • Gain actionable insights from predictive analytics.
  • Simulate different scenarios to understand potential impacts on liquidity and risk.

4. Treasury Innovations

The 2022 releases also focus on treasury-specific enhancements, such as:

  • Integration with Multi-Bank Connectivity (MBC): Enabling seamless communication with multiple banking partners.
  • Improved Cash Pooling: Supporting better management of cash across subsidiaries and regions.
  • Risk Management Enhancements: Including tools to better manage foreign exchange, interest rate, and commodity risks.

Corporate Treasury’s Role in Turbulent Times

Corporate treasurers play a vital role in maintaining stability and driving strategic financial decisions. In uncertain times, they must focus on:

  • Risk Mitigation: Ensuring that risks are identified, quantified, and managed effectively.
  • Capital Efficiency: Optimizing the use of available capital to enhance returns.
  • Collaboration: Working closely with other business units to align financial strategies with organizational goals.

Key Highlights of the 2022 Releases

Christian Mnich, an expert in SAP Treasury and WCM, highlighted the following innovations in the 2022 releases:

  • Comprehensive integration of SAP S/4HANA with the Taulia platform.
  • Enhanced tools for liquidity forecasting and cash management.
  • Advanced analytics capabilities to optimize working capital metrics.

For more detailed insights, you can explore the dedicated SAP blog or watch the topic-specific video highlights on YouTube: SAP Treasury and WCM Innovations 2022.

Conclusion

SAP’s continuous investments in Treasury and Working Capital Management demonstrate its commitment to empowering organizations to navigate economic challenges and thrive. The 2022 SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud releases provide advanced tools for optimizing liquidity, managing risks, and improving working capital efficiency. By leveraging these innovations, organizations can ensure they remain resilient and competitive in an ever-changing business landscape.

SAP Community  SAP S/4HANA #Treasury Management SAP Risk Management for SAP S/4HANA SAP Treasury and Risk Management SAP Cash Application #SAP

entry.title: Re: Order cannot be delivered (see long text)
entry.content:
Dear 

First check the Copy control setting as suggested by the other team membor.

 

Please check the Delivery Type in tcode OVLK under the tab order reference " It should be set  ''Sales order required.

may be someone has changed the order reference as a No Proceeding docuemnt required.

Reward if you found this ok.

Regards,

Lalit Nagar

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for Java 8.10 on the Horizon
entry.content:
As mentioned before:

If you have specific problems, please report them as support incidents to the BC-FES-JAV component. The type of information required is described in Note 326558 (Error description in SAP GUI for Java).

For how to submit support incidents see note 1296527 .

We are not able to reproduce the issue from the information you provided. In the incident, please describe exact steps and particular source, where to put the cursor before doing the pretty print etc. We also might need to log on to your system.

entry.title: Re: Service Order with Procurement of Spare Parts from Intercompany Supplier
entry.content:
That looks very good. Nice work around to the limitation for account assigned advanced shipment with interco.

You could add the process with serial number, as this is quite common in service

entry.title: Re: How to add Snowflake as a source for SAP Datasphere or SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @Sefan_Linders ,

We are experiencing a performance issue while creating virtual tables using Snowflake SDI. It is taking over 8 minutes to create a single virtual table.

Upon investigation, we noticed that Snowflake executes the following commands during the creation process:

show /* JDBC:DatabaseMetaData.getPrimaryKeys() */ primary keys in account
show /* JDBC:DatabaseMetaData.getColumns() */ columns in account

Each command takes more than 4 minutes to execute. While there are more efficient methods to retrieve columns and primary keys of a table, these commands seem to be scanning the entire account instead of focusing on the specific table.

Is there any adjustment needed at the HANA or agent level to optimize these queries so that they retrieve details only for the relevant table?

Thanks in advance for your assistance!

entry.title: Re: Peering Options for SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi @rodfior,

Services like Cloud Connector can also leverage from the improved bandwidth and potentially latency that can be achieved with such a setup. It does not have an impact on the configuration and setup of the Cloud Connector or similar Connectivity Services. We just shorten the network path, we do not change packages.

entry.title: Re: Import Sales Orders - Part4: Custom fields support
entry.content:
Hi @lauris-gale, it's possible. Find your existing custom fields in app Custom Field(F1481) and to enable the usage "SD_SALES_ORDER_IMPORT", then the custom fields can be used in Import Sales Orders app.

All existing custom fields in Business Context: Sales Document (SD_SALESDOC) & Sales Document Item(SD_SALESDOCITEM) are supported.

entry.title: #ABAP_trial Steps to install SAP NetWeaver 7.52 SP04 TestDrive on Linux (RHEL 8/VirtualBox)
entry.content:
Hello,

I just created this post showing how to install SAP NetWeaver 7.52 SP04 TestDrive on a RedHat Enterprise Linux 8 system running on VirtualBox.

I hope you find it useful.

Regards

Jochen

entry.title: Re: Alternate resources (via WC classification)not creati...

I will however try to replicate steps again and hopefully get to the final result of getting alternate mode in the PDS. Thanks again and i will come back with the results.

entry.title: Re: How to Convert from HTML to PDF in SAP CPI - Part I
entry.content:
Dinu, thank you for your comment.

As far as I know, they are not discontinuing it.

I even added a note regarding it, I was alerted to the usage of SAP Forms service. We had some constraints by the customer, so we were not able to explore the usage of such a service, but we will for sure recommend it next time something like this comes up.

entry.title: Re: Meet your new friend EMIS: Event Mesh in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
@yannickb89 

Let me start with a short answer: SAP Cloud Application Event Hub is geared towards SAP Cloud Applications as the name indicates (for once an SAP name actually fully fits). It does not support any on prem backends or 3rd party components. 

Event Mesh and Advanced Event Mesh are event brokers/meshes offering basic and (highly) advanced features in the SAP ecosystem and beyond. So this includes on prem and 3rd party. Specifically AEM has been developed to be the backbone of EDA across the entire enterprise, way beyond just SAP. 

Important point: all these offerings complement each other. So the Event Hub can be used as an event hub for SAP Cloud Applications and feed these events into AEM/EM (feature on the roadmap for early 2025), for example. 

I would recommend to read this longer document describing SAP's EDA strategy. It provides a full overview of how the pieces fit together.

 Best,

Karsten

entry.title: User-exit to redetermine Collection Group in SD-Billing
entry.content:
Hi expert:

 

I understand that we can set up “Collection Group” in customer master.

 

Any user-exit that can redetermine collection group when we generated SD-billing that we create FI document to AR?

entry.title: Sell from Subcontractor - With Shipment Processing Part 2
entry.content:
Business Background

The sell-from-subcontractor cycle is a strategic solution that can be employed by companies to address manufacturing capacity constraints while ensuring efficient fulfilment of customer demands. In this process, raw materials are sent to a subcontractor, who leverages their facilities and expertise to manufacture finished goods in accordance with the company’s specifications. These goods are then maintained in customer consignment stock under the supplier’s ownership, providing flexibility and readiness for customer orders. When a customer demand arises, the company strategically authorizes the subcontractor to ship the finished products directly to the customer, bypassing internal logistics and minimizing lead times. This streamlined approach not only alleviates manufacturing bottlenecks but also enhances operational efficiency, reduces costs, and ensures prompt delivery. Leveraging SAP functionalities, such as subcontracting purchase orders, consignment stock management, and delivery processes, this cycle provides end-to-end visibility and control, enabling companies to maintain high levels of customer satisfaction and operational excellence. Below is the value chain diagram of the scenario.

Table of Content

  1. Customer Consignment Fill-up & Subcontracting Outbound Delivery Schedule Lines
  2. Requirement Class
  3. Sales Order Cost Estimate
  4. Customer Consignment Fill-up Pricing Procedure
  5. Subcontracting Delivery
  6. Route Determination for Outbound Delivery - Raw Materials
  7. Goods Issue on Shipment Completion
  8. Split Valuation
  9. Stock Determination

Visit https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-for-industrial-manufacturing-blogs/sell-from-subcontractor-with-shipment-processing-part-1/ba-p/13976975 for the Master Data & Business Processes Guide.

Configuration

The following configuration steps will be discussed to ensure the seamless execution of the sell-from-subcontractor process in SAP. First, the schedule line category for customer consignment will be configured to enable efficient planning and fulfilment of consignment stock. Next, the schedule line category for subcontracting outbound deliveries will be addressed to facilitate smooth delivery processes. Following this, the requirement class will be set up to define the necessary planning and procurement strategies. The configuration of the sales order cost estimate will then be covered to ensure accurate costing in the customer consignment fill-up process. Additionally, we will discuss the setup for the shipment of raw materials to the subcontractor, including movement types and handling requirements ensuring a fully integrated and traceable workflow across procurement and delivery stages. Finally, the configuration for split valuation and stock determination will be reviewed to enable precise inventory management and ensure optimal stock handling throughout the process. Note, New sales order type, delivery order types, and item categories have been copied from the standard type not to affect the standard types in terms of determination.

1. Customer Consignment Fill-up & Subcontracting Outbound Delivery Schedule Lines

The first schedule line to be configured, is the schedule line of the consignment fill-up. Go to the following path Sales and Distribution>Sales>Sales Documents>Schedule Lines>Define Schedule Line Categories or use Tcode VOV6. Take a copy of E0 schedule line. Add the purchase requisition type, item category 3, and account assignment category M. Note, a new item category was created, item category ZKBN, which is a copy from KBN for this scenario to be managed separately.

 

2. The second schedule line to be configured, is the schedule line that of the outbound delivery to be generated from the subcontracting purchase order. Take a copy of schedule line category LB. Change the movement type from 541 to 30A, thus, enabling the raw materials to be in transit upon issuing the goods. Note, a new item category was created, item category ZLBN, which is a copy from LBN for this scenario to be managed separately.

2. Requirement Class

1. The Requirement class will be responsible from where the stock is to be issued (Sales Order stock for example) and how the valuation of the finished goods will be managed in the scenario. Create a new requirement class using the following path Sales and Distribution>Basic Functions>Availability Check and Transfer of Requirements>Transfer of Requirements>Configure Requirement Classes or use Tcode OVZG. Under account assignment, add account assignment category M.

a. The account assignment category M is chosen as the sales order will not be carrying costs and revenue, meaning that no settlement of costs is needed on the sales order, this is controlled by leaving the consumption posting field blank in Tcode OME9 (Check Account Assignment Categories). Moreover, the special stock indicator should be E to be able to issue the finished goods to customer consignment after the goods receipt on the subcontracting purchase order from the sales order stock.

2. After setting up the requirement class, go to the following path Sales and Distribution>Basic Functions>Account Assignment/Costing>Transfer of Requirements>Maintain Requirement Classes for Costing/Account Assignment to set up how the costs are to be managed in the customer consignment fill-up. First, is the account assignment category, which is M, assigned in the requirement class. Second, is the valuation M. This indicator is used to indicate that the valuation of the materials in sales order will be valued differently from the standard valuation price of the material manufactured at the company. For the costing ID, add B which means that the sales order will be automatically costed and marked upon saving the sales order. Costing method 1 is used as the costing type used in this scenario is product cost. Meaning that the costs of the finished goods will be brought automatically based on the material's BOM, Routes, and Supplier Info record. Add costing variant PPC4, more explanation on this costing variant will be discussed in the upcoming section. Finally, add condition type line items EK01, as this condition will be the only condition type used in consignment fill-up pricing procedure to be populated from the total value of the cost estimate.

3. Create a new requirement type and assign it to the requirement class from the previous point by going to Sales and Distribution>Basic Functions>Availability Check and Transfer of Requirements>Transfer of Requirements>Configure Requirement Types or use Tcode OVZH.

4. Finally, go to the following path Sales and Distribution>Basic Functions>Availability Check and Transfer of Requirements>Transfer of Requirements>Determine Requirement Type by Item Category and MRP Type or use Tcode OVZI to assign the requirement type to the item category assigned consignment fill-up order type. The 1 under source requirement type means that the requirement class will be determined from item category and not from the material master.

3. Sales Order Cost Estimate

1. The sales order cost estimate is maintained under the following path Controlling>Product Cost Controlling>Cost Object Controlling>Product Cost by Sales Order> Preliminary Costing and Order BOM Costing> Product Costing for Sales Order Items/Order BOMs>Costing Variants for Product Costing>Check Costing Variants for Product Costing or use Tcode OKY9. Using the sales order cost estimate you can control multiple things, including the determination of the BOM (raw materials that make up the finished goods) and routing (Activities such as labour cost per hour) in the sales order cost estimate. To further view the determination of BOM and routing, press on Qty Struct. Control.

a. Press on BOM Application under BOM to view the BOM determination or Routing Selection under Routing to view the routing determination.

b. BOM Selection based on priority, usage, and minimum BOM requirement that is needed in order for BOM to be derived in the sales order cost estimate.

c. Route usage based on priority, task list, plan usage, and status of the routing to be derived in the cost estimate based on the below determination.

2. Note that you need to assign the primary cost elements to the cost component structure, such as for materials consumption and third party maintained in the material account determination (OBYC). This is done in the following path Controlling>Product Cost Controlling>Cost Object Controlling>Product Cost by Sales Order> Preliminary Costing and Order BOM Costing> Product Costing for Sales Order Items/Order BOMs>Define Cost Components or use Tcode OKTZ.

a. Choose the active version of cost component structure.

b. Choose the desired cost component attribute (e.g. Direct Material or Third Party).

 

c. Assign the cost component to cost element G/L interval that is maintained in your chart of accounts.

4. Customer Consignment Fill-up Pricing Procedure

Below is the pricing schema for the consignment fill-up. Condition EK01 is the same condition type added in the requirement class.

5. Subcontracting Delivery

1. Make a copy from delivery type LB in 0VLK (Define Delivery Types) then go to Materials Management> Purchasing>Purchase Order>Set Up Subcontract Order or use Tcode OMGM and assign the newly created delivery type, ZLB delivery type, to the supplying plant. The supplying plant is then to be added to the supplier's customer role in business partner, as this is how the determination of the delivery type will be derived.

2. To use the schedule line maintained in point 2 in section Customer Consignment Fill-up & Subcontracting Outbound Delivery Schedule lines, the determination of the delivery item category needs to be maintained first. Go to Logistics Execution> Shipping>Deliveries>Define Item Category Determination in Deliveries or use Tcode 0184. In the determination, make sure that the usage used is V as it relates to purchase order. Assignment of item category to schedule line can be then maintained through Tcode VOV5 (Assign Schedule Line Categories).

 

6. Route Determination for Outbound Delivery - Raw Materials

1. First, maintain transportation relevance for the delivery type of the subcontracting, delivery type ZLB that is mentioned in the previous section, under Logistics Execution>Transportation>Shipments>Shipments>Maintain Transportation Relevance or use Tcode V_TVLK_TR.

 

2. Second, maintain transportation relevance for the delivery item category type of the subcontracting, delivery type ZLB that is mentioned in the previous section, under Logistics Execution>Transportation>Shipments>Shipments>Maintain Transportation Relevance or use Tcode V_TVLP_TL.

 

3. Third, maintain route transportation relevance under Logistics Execution>Transportation>Shipments>Shipments>Maintain Transportation Relevance use Tcode V_TVRO.

 

4. Finally, the route is determined based on the Route Determination with Weight Group (Delivery). Go to Logistics Execution>Transportation>Basic Transportation Functions>Routes>Route Determination>Maintain Route Determination or use Tcode 0VRF. Select the desired departure and receiver zone in step 1 and go to Route Determination with Weight Group (Delivery) step 2, that is because the route here will be determined in delivery and not in order (sales order).

5. Add the route determination as below. Make sure that the weight group to be chosen is 9999. The weight group is determined based on the total weight of the outbound delivery.

 

a. The weight group is maintained under Route Determination with Weight Group (Delivery). Go to Logistics Execution>Transportation>Basic Transportation Functions>Routes>Route Determination>Define Weight Groups. Here the standard weight group 9999 is maintained.

 

b. After maintaining the weight group, the standard is designed that the total weight is a large dummy weight as seen below.

 

7. Goods Issue on Shipment Completion

1. For the sake of this scenario, upon pressing shipment completion in the shipment document (goods in the delivery document to be issued from the company), the following configuration was implemented. Go to the following path Logistics Execution>Transportation>Shipments>Define and Assign Activity Profiles or use Tcode 0VTL. Add Z variant then press on maintain.

 

2. Select Carry out goods issue posting during save then save the variant.

 

8. Split Valuation

1. The split valuation is used in this scenario as to split the valuation price cost of the finished product manufactured at company site and the finished product manufactured at subcontractor site. First, you need to activate the split valuation using the following path Materials Management>Valuation and Account Assignment>Split Valuation>Activate Split Valuation or use Tcode OMW0.

 

2. Next, go to Materials Management>Valuation and Account Assignment>Split Valuation>Configure Split Valuation or use Tcode OMWC. Choose Global Types.

 

3. Maintain the following two valuation types. Valuation type ZEXTER-SC represents the valuation price of the subcontractor and ZINTER-FG represents the valuation price of the internal manufactured goods. For both EXT.POs and Int.POs, three options are available which are:

  • if the valuation type can't be used for external/internal (STO) purchase orders, which is defined as 0.
  • if the valuation type can be used for external/internal (STO) purchase orders but with warning, which is defined as 1.
  • if the valuation type is allowed be used for external/internal (STO) purchase orders, which is defined as 2.

Finally, the account category reference represents a group for the valuation class assigned to the material master.

 

a. Press on Account Cat. Ref. to view the assigned valuation classes assigned to the Account Category Reference.

 

 

b. To edit the below assignment, go to the following path Materials Management>Valuation and Account Assignment>Account Determination>Account Determination Without Wizard>Define Valuation Classes or use Tcode OMSK and press on Valuation Class

 

4. Next step is to define the valuation category. Press on Global Categories.

 

5. The global category is used to define which valuation types can be defaulted when processing external or internal procurement. Both flags, external procurement (ExP) and Inhouse production (Inh), are used for not allowing the valuation type to be overtyped in the procurement process. Press on Types->Cat.

6. Activate both newly created valuation types for the Z valuation category.

7. Final step is to activate the valuation category on plant level. Press on Local Definitions.

8. Select the plant and press on Cats.->OU.

9. Select the valuation category and press on activate.

9. Stock Determination

1. The stock determination is used in this scenario and that is because before any goods issue of outbound delivery for materials that are relevant for split valuation, the valuation type must be entered at either sales order or outbound delivery. This process can be automated when the outbound delivery is subjected to goods issue for non-special stock, meaning it can be automated for normal sales and not sales from consignment stock for example. Other than that, it needs to be added manually. Go to the following path Materials Management>Inventory Management and Physical Inventory>Stock Determination>Define Strategies for Stock Determination or use Tcode OSPX. First, create stock determination group as seen below.

2. Create stock determination rule.

 

3. Go stock determination Header Table and maintain the combination between the plant to be used for this scenario, the created stock determination group, and the stock determination rule.

a. Double click on the previously created stock determination header table and maintain the below settings.

4. Go to the item level of the stock determination header of the previous step and maintain the below. The F under the special stock indicator column represents the unrestricted stock of the company. It is mandatory to add priority, add 1. Finally, add the valuation type of the internal manufactured goods (created in the split valuation section point 3).

 

5. Finally, go to Materials Management>Inventory Management and Physical Inventory>Stock Determination>Assign Stock Determination Rule in the Applications>Delivery to assign the created stock determination rule for the delivery item category of the normal sales or consignment fill-up other than the consignment fill-up order used in this scenario.

entry.title: Re: Updating Short text of Inspection Lot using BO Interface - I_INSPECTIONLOTTP_2 (EML/RAP)
entry.content: @Jose3Is there any error in "ls_update_reported"?

entry.title: IC universal allocation set up from PS perspective
entry.content:
Summary

This article includes the following topics connected with IC universal allocation set up from PS perspective:

1. Creation of  new sub cost element group dedicated to the new process

2. Configuration of the material, which will be used for the IC universal allocation cycle

3. Tax classification  set up of the material

4. Set up of the material in the IC Billing profile

Creation of  new sub cost element group dedicated to the new process

1. Creation of a sub cost element group in the Cost element group YBPS_BILLI in the app Edit Cost Element Groups.

2. There should be included the requested secondary expense G/L accounts. In the current case these are: 

Configuration of the material, which will be used for the IC universal allocation cycle

The material set up includes:

1. In the tab (1) Distribution Chains of the app Manage Product Master Data, should be set up the (2)Delivery Plant:

 

If this is not configured when you start the job Generate intercompany billing request, the following error “Plant could not be derived” will appear:

2. There should be created (1) valuation areas in the product master data:

In the valuation areas should be chosen the respective valuation class (2). The valuation class determines the account for cost postings, which at the end, determines if the system finds a material for the billing process.

If a new G/L expense account is requested for creation, it should have specific characteristics in order to be used for IDOC transfer.

For the new G/L expense account should be created a new valuation class by the MM team.

SSUI 102463 Assign G/L Accounts in CBC should be used in order the expenses to be posted to the correct G/L expense account.

If a different revenue G/L account is requested to be set up, there should be checked SSUI 100297 Automatic Account Determination in CBC for its settings:

  • Area

Area: Sales

Subarea: Sales Billing

Process: Assign G/L Accounts

  • Parameters:

Chart of Accounts: YCOA

Application: V

Condition table: 001

Sales org.:  respective sales organization to be filled

In the current case for G/L account (1) 42000013 the account assignment material group is (2) Z5. This account assignment should be added to the material.

The following settings should be done:

  1. Go to app Change Material:

2. Chose the view Sales: Sales Org. Data 2:

3. Go to tab Org. Levels:

4. Fill the data for Plant, Sales Org., Distr. Channel and press the button Continue:

for the material and save the data:

Tax classification  set up of the material

1. There should be checked in the app Set Service Prices - Sales the Material tax classification for the respective country:

  • Chose condition type TTX1 :

  • Chose (1)departure and (2)destination country/region:

  • Chose key combination Export Taxes

  • Check for the column Tax classification material (1) the rule Liable for TaxeFull Tax Services (2).

2. Set the proper tax classification of the material in the app Change material, view Sales: sales org.1, section Tax data:

Set up of the material in the IC Billing profile

1. Go to SSUI 103637 Maintain Billing Profile in CBC and chose Billing Profile for Intercompany Billing:

2. For the new Material, which is expense type the material determination should include:

  • Set up based on a  G/L account number:

  • Set up based on a Material Number:

entry.title: Re: New AIF features with SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2302

Hi Alex 

I have question regarding message dashboard app in AIF in S/4HANA Public cloud. It also shows email notification but when i get any error than only alert comes , no email . Is this functionality available in S/4 HANA cloud. is there any way to achieve same if user wants email notification for every error apart from Alert in their my inbox of fiori portal. 

Thanks 

Amit Gupta

entry.title: Re: ABAP Dump Error

entry.title: Re: RECADV not available in WE81
entry.content:
Hello @flaviolemos_prio 

It looks like Proof of Delivery at Goods Receipt for Inbound Deliveries. It is transmitted to the vendor with STPPOD message.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Consume a REST Service on Cloud Application Studio
entry.content:
Hello fceylan,

I hope you are reading this. As I can see in your Post you were sending a GET request to a webservice to gain some information. I am trying the same, but unfortunately the content-element of the RESTCallResult is empty. I can see that the request was done successfully since "RequestUrl" and "Code" are filled and the Response Code is 200.

For every other webservice request I did so far, the content element was always filled with some xml or json data the webservice responded. I am sure that my current webservice, which Request Method is GET, responds with the data. I've checked it over and over again. Hope you have some ideas, what am I doing wrong.

Regards

Saskia

entry.title: Rebate Amount Based on two Different Target Quantities| Condition Contract | Settlement Management
entry.content:
We have a business requirement where the rebate needs to be applied on the basis of two different conditions.

Example : Customer is only eligible for the Rebate if there has been a purchase of 300 EA of Material A.

If the Cutsomer has achieved the 300 EA target of Material A, a Rebate of 5 USD/EA needs to be provided for purchasing upto 100 EA of Material B

If the customer invoices are 400 EA of Material A, 70 EA of Material B -->Rebate Amount = 5 * 70 = 350 

If the customer invoices are 200 EA of Material A, 70 EA of Material B -->Rebate Amount = 0

If the customer invoices are 200 EA of Material A, 150 EA of Material B -->Rebate Amount = 5*100=500

I have only seen that using scales we can set up a rebate to be applicable based on a quantity of one material. Is the above scenario possible with standard configuration?

entry.title: Re: Implementer's Guide to Rapid Financial Planning & Analysis for SAP S/4HANA using SAP Analyti
entry.content: NEW! You can now request to join the Rapid FP&A WorkZone (for recordings and presentations from historical webinars) here: https://workzone-core.one.int.sap/groups/BGiy9l0qOsEfjxJwAxQBLi

entry.title: RFUI in EWM Best Practices?
entry.content: Why are all (?) Best practices run with RFUI for the embedded EWM steps? Are there any best practices that use "fiori/backend" apps instead? Better to start with these for education.....

entry.title: Re: Exploring CPI's filesystem's content
entry.content:
Hi Ariel, very elegant solution you provided there.

Still, there is one thing I cannot wrap my head around: how do I authenticate via the browser onto my IFlow Endpoint, as the Role ESBMessaging.send uses OAuth 2? 

Is there a simple way to authenticate the browser, or somehow trigger basic Auth?
Or would it be easier to configure a Client Certificate?

Best regards
Aris

entry.title: Re: to unlock the locked group material data
entry.content:
Please check Tcode . SM12... you can see there the locks on SAP Objects and clear them out.

Reward.

Lalit Nagar

entry.title: Re: Custom Compliance Form setup - anyone used this yet?
entry.content:
@Niladri_B_Nayak 

We would use it for moving the Custom Forms Signing step to a parallel process to avoid this step blocking for the User Data to flow to MPH for further processing.
My understanding is that utilizing Custom Compliance Forms instead of the standard Document Flow step would do just this, and still have e-signing with DocuSign etc running in its own process similar to the Standard Compliance Processes that comes out of the box.

Can you verify this please?

Are there any limitation to number of dynamic data fields etc for Custom Compliance Forms or other limitations we need to be aware of?

Really appreciate your input on this - thanks.

entry.title: Re: grouping radio buttons on screen painter
entry.content: This solved for me when adding a new one to existing ones. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Food for thought: What’s your favorite holiday dish?
entry.content:
@MariettaLopez  Thank a lot for sharing:)! I was imagining the dishes as I was reading your comment. Thank you!:) It was delicious 🙂

Before university I was working in a ski resort in the states and I was invited to a Thanksgiving dinner and I loved the Turkey and gravy sauce they served:). If you happen to have pictures of the dishes, I would love to see them:).

Happy new year:)

entry.title: Re: There's nothing to show yet - Compensation Portlet
entry.content:
Hi @angelosuano 

I did see https://me.sap.com/notes/3542411 you shared with me in KBA and am checking with my colleagues from Product.

In the meantime can you please help with the following: 

  • confirm whether what is in KBA (PCG design) applies to your environment ?
  • share screenshots of each page for the design of this tile (assuming it's a custom one similar to the one I built in this blog)
  • is this a new tile you just created or did it work before ?
  • share a screenshot of the salary review template Summary section in admin center (where we can how many forms are in each step).

Often this happens if there are no forms launched for the template, or if they are all completed, or if there is simply a bad design of the tile. I believe there is more information in the blog linked above.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: CREATE button is not appearing after deployed Fiori A...
entry.content: @saurabh__khare : HI, I was able to see the Create Button in deployed application but another problem is when i click on CREATE button it is not navigating to Next Object Page

entry.title: Re: CTMS Transport fails
entry.content:
Hi @Sandhya2 ,

Sorry to hear that you are facing issues with the deployment!

As you might know, "Check Connection" does not always provide meaningful results for destinations using OAuth2Password authentication, but as it does fit to the deploy error, this does not apply here.

Just to make sure: as it did work with another user, I am sure that the URL does fit to the following convention:
https://deploy-service.cf.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/slprot/977a24d6-2eaf-432d-a3e1-5294451551a3/slp
(as often, "/slp" is missing), correct?

The user also has to fulfill the following prerequisites (as listed in Creating Destinations Using SAP Cloud Deployment Service with OAuth2Password Authentication | SAP Help Portal😞

  • The user used for the destination must be a valid user on Cloud Foundry environment and it must have the role SpaceDeveloper in the target space.

  • The user must be a platform user so that the deployment works for all content types. For more information, see Platform Users.

  • The user used for the destination isn’t subject to any Data Protection and Privacy requirements.

  • We recommend that you use a technical user to avoid constraints typically associated with personal users, such as password rotation.

If it should not work out, consider to switch the destination to using SAP Content Agent service instead of the deploy service, as described here: Creating Destinations Using SAP Content Agent Service | SAP Help Portal
This has the advantage that it is token-based, so no user/password is required.

Best regards,
Boris

entry.title: Principal propagation through AWS Application Load Balancer (ALB)
entry.content:
Hello,

Can you please help in answering the following questions.

  1. The prerequisites mention that for identity propagation, mutual authentication (mTLS) is mandatory, then how is the case 1 of SAP note 3482634 applicable when mtls is disabled on the load balancer.
  2. "As per SAP note 3482634, If SAP Cloud Connector version is lower than 2.17.0, we need to disable ECDHE, DHE ciphersuites using property jdk.tls.disabledAlgorithms in file $JAVA_HOME/jre/lib/security/java.security (on unix/linux). Can you please help me understand why this is required?" 

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Principal propagation through AWS Application Load Ba...
entry.content:
Hello,

Can you please help in answering the following questions.

  1. The prerequisites mention that for identity propagation, mutual authentication (mTLS) is mandatory, then how is the case 1 of SAP note 3482634 applicable when mtls is disabled on the load balancer.
  2. "As per SAP note 3482634, If SAP Cloud Connector version is lower than 2.17.0, we need to disable ECDHE, DHE ciphersuites using property jdk.tls.disabledAlgorithms in file $JAVA_HOME/jre/lib/security/java.security (on unix/linux). Can you please help me understand why this is required?"

entry.title: Re: Towards Test-Driven Development with SAP Process Integration Test Tool
entry.content:
Hi @anna_winterholler 

Now the same concept can be used with SAP Integration Suite programs: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/test-driven-development-for-sap-integration-suite-migrations-a-game-changer/ba-p/13973592

Best Regards,

Michal Krawczyk

entry.title: Are you looking for an overview of the SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Discovering Response & Supply for SAP IBP – order-based planning learning journey.

Overview
In this course, you will explore the essential features and functionalities of the order-based planning business processes within SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) for response and supply. You will get an introduction, how to leverages historical and real-time order data to create accurate supply plans that are directly linked to customer orders and sales forecasts. This approach enables businesses to focus on fulfilling specific customer requirements, taking into account order priorities, inventory levels, lead times, and available supply resources.

Learning objectives
After completing this course, learners will be able to describe the key business processes and explain the the benefits of the order-based planning in the SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) for response and supply planning business application.

Prerequisites

  • Exploring Planning Processes in SAP IBP
  • Exploring the Tools and Features in SAP IBP
     

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Rebate Amount Based on two Different Target Quantitie...
entry.content:
Hi @priya_sshekhar, 

The scenario you described involves a product mix condition for rebates, which is not supported out of the box by SAP CCM through standard configuration. Currently, SAP CCM allows for rebate conditions to be set up based on quantities, values, or scales for individual materials, but it does not natively support scenarios where rebates depend on a combination of conditions across multiple materials.

To address this requirement, you have the following options:

  1. Custom Development: This would involve developing custom logic to evaluate the combined conditions (e.g., checking the purchase quantity of Material A to determine eligibility and then applying the rebate for Material B accordingly). This custom logic can be integrated into the condition contract creation process or the settlement process.

  2. Manual Management: Alternatively, the eligibility and rebate criteria could be managed manually by validating the conditions before creating the condition contract or before executing the settlement process. While this avoids custom development, it requires additional manual effort and could increase the risk of errors.

If custom development is pursued, collaboration with an experienced SAP developer or consultant is recommended to ensure the solution integrates seamlessly with your existing CCM setup.

I hope this helps clarify your options.

Regards, 
Gauthier

entry.title: Re: Empty content in RESTCallResult for GET when using Ex...
entry.content:
Nevermind! I solved it on my own and the solution is:

The debugger "Locals" window did not show any result, but when I traced the information it was clearly shown in the "Output" window. Just lost one day on this...

entry.title: Issue with Automatic Deduction for Semi-Finished Goods Using Movement Type 531
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I have a scenario where we are using the same material code (let's call it "1") as both a finished product and a semi-finished product according to its specification.

Here's the situation:

  • We want to automatically deduct a quantity of this material for another plant in CO15 using movement type 531.
  • In the specification, we assigned a special procurement type "70" to this material. However, we cannot set this in the material master because the material code is also used as a finished product.

Example:
If we confirm 10 pieces:

  • 5 pieces should be deducted for the other plant using movement type 531.
  • The remaining 5 pieces should be received at the production plant.

Problem:
Currently, SAP is receiving all 10 pieces at the production plant instead of deducting 5 for the other plant.

Question:
How can we configure this process so that SAP automatically deducts the quantity sent to the other plant during goods receipt?

Thank you in advance for your help!

entry.title: SAM2110 Synchronization Start Time Stamp
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there any standard tables, apart from the below provided table names, where the entire application synchronization start timestamp is updated.

/SYCLO/MDW01 – Open sync session and Close sync session timestamp is available.

CDHDR – The post transaction timestamp of each entity is available. 

So, kindly let us know if there is any standard table which captures the sync start time irrespective of with/without changes?

Thanks

Kiran

entry.title: Re: Consume a REST Service on Cloud Application Studio
entry.content: Nevermind! I solved it on my own and the solution is: The debugger "Locals" window did not show any result, but when I traced the information it was clearly shown in the "Output" window. Just lost one day on this...

entry.title: Not able to create goods issue with BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE with 351 mvt type
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

when I am trying to create goods issue using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE with movement type 351 against STO (Stock transport order) and I am getting error message saying, 'Goods movement not possible with mvmt type 351'. kindly help me.

Code:

lwa_goodsmvt_header-pstng_date = '20250108'.
lwa_goodsmvt_header-doc_date = '20250108'.
*lwa_goodsmvt_header- = '70002413'.
st_goodsmvt_code-gm_code = '03'.

*Item data
lwa_goodsmvt_item-material = 'PB500145'.
lwa_goodsmvt_item-po_number = '70002413'.
lwa_goodsmvt_item-po_item = '00001'.
lwa_goodsmvt_item-entry_qnt = '1'.
lwa_goodsmvt_item-entry_uom = 'EA'.
* lwa_goodsmvt_item-batch = <lwa_itm>-charg.
lwa_goodsmvt_item-prod_date = '20250108'.
lwa_goodsmvt_item-move_type = '351'.
lwa_goodsmvt_item-plant = 'XXXX'. "Hiding plant
*lwa_goodsmvt_item-stge_loc = '0001'.
lwa_goodsmvt_item-mvt_ind = lc_b.
lwa_goodsmvt_item-stck_type = 'F'.
APPEND lwa_goodsmvt_item TO lt_goodsmvt_item.
CLEAR: lwa_goodsmvt_item.

CLEAR:lw_mblnr.

* BAPI for Post Goods Receipt
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'
EXPORTING
goodsmvt_header = lwa_goodsmvt_header
goodsmvt_code = st_goodsmvt_code
IMPORTING
materialdocument = lw_mblnr
TABLES
goodsmvt_item = lt_goodsmvt_item
return = lt_ret.

READ TABLE lt_ret INTO lwa_return WITH KEY type = lc_e.
IF sy-subrc EQ 0.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'.
lwa_return-type = lc_e.
APPEND lwa_return TO lt_return.
CLEAR: lwa_return.

ELSE.
* saving Goods issue
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
EXPORTING
wait = lc_x.

endif.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Configuring Automatic Performance Form Launch on Hiring and Rehiring
entry.content:
Hi Abhijeet,

 

Thanks for your Informative Blog . I have also configured  Form launch based on Job Change event through Business Rules. But the form is not launched. Could you please help if there will be any provisioning job that needs to be configured for the form launch.

 

Regards

Aradhana

entry.title: Re: Goals Rating through hyperlink in VarPay form
entry.content:
Hi @Konstantina 

It's worst practice to do that but it is possible (I've built it for a customer before that insisted despite our warnings).

The only limitation is that the Performance and the Variable Pay forms must both be in the inbox of the person you want to be able to edit the goals ratings at the same time. 

See this presentation I made for the customer back in 2018 and that I anonymized.

All the best,
Xavier

entry.title: Re: Enable faster user changes and shared terminals using badges in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content: @Alexand3r for a shared terminal, a shared user would be needed. Br, Manoel

entry.title: BW on HANA service pack upgrade impact - High importance
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are planning to upgrade the BW system for 7.5 SPS 6 to SPS 30 or 31. Will there be any impact on existing BW and HANA models?

And we do have current BPC plugin as below version. Will it support or any impact when we upgrade the BW?

SAP BUSINESS PLANNING AND CONS  800  SPS 15 (10/14)  sap.com  SBOP PC 10.0 FOR SAP NW

 

Thanks,

Prabhakar

entry.title: SAP HANA Service pack upgrade - High importance
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are planning to upgrade  HANA 2.0 SP 5 to 2.0 SPS 7 or higher or SPS 8. Will there be any Impact on existing BW and HANA models?

 

Thanks,

Prabhakar

entry.title: S/4HANA CM - CRM: Competitor Products
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

In S/4HANA Customer Management, I want to create a competitor product within the opportunity document. However, the competitor product creation component and the "New" button available in R3 are missing. Additionally, how can I associate the competitor product with the competitor?

Thanks for feedback.

entry.content: The client query is, AUC is capitalized.. again some of the asset value wants to add in the same AUC through PO & GRN after month or days. Is it possible to do...

entry.title: Manage Withholding Tax Items missing in Run Statutory reports app
entry.content:
Manage Withholding Tax Items is missing in Run Statutory reports app.

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Manage Withholding Tax Items missing in Run Statutory...
entry.content: See kba 3503880 No app Manage Withholding Tax Items in search result

entry.title: Re: Creating Custom Client IDs in SAP Document Informatio...
entry.content:
Hi Sundar,
As I stated in my last reply, you need to add the schema name only and not the field names. Please refer to the options I posted in my previous reply as an example.

options = {"schemaName":"Name of your schema","clientId":"your client id","documentType":"custom (the document type you used to create your schema)"}


Best Tobias

entry.title: Re: Able to get list all components while booking Non con...
entry.content: Hi @nehadwivedi, by design, only assembled components show up out of the box at the moment. Br, Manoel

entry.title: Re: (Re)using Master Operations in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Thanks @ManoelCosta, this topic is one of the most crucial things to understand and leverage in modelling the execution process, especially as it enables the reuse of assigned objects (Data Collection Groups, Equipment Set Points ...) and enables the setting of Resource Types and default Resources, which is a core prerequisite for using the Scheduling features (Resource Orchestration).

This is something I stress in every implementation and template I'm involved with.

entry.title: Re: Creating PYTD at Day granularity in SAC new model
entry.content: hi @PM8 , could you find a solution about it? have the same issue doin it in model for the pydt but stuck. any help would be appricated

entry.title: Re: Scanning in app "Manage Physical Inventory Count" doe...
entry.content:
Hi @Hansani_H

No, not yet...

Bruno

entry.title: Re: SAP Content Server maintenance date
entry.content: Hello,

entry.title: Re: (Re)using Master Operations in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content: Indeed, @michael_koerner3. 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAP Content Server maintenance date
entry.content: Hi. Quick follow-up question. Note https://me.sap.com/notes/719971 does indeed confirm that Content Server will be supported with S/4HANA until 2040. However, how will the Content Server updates be delivered, since they were previously packaged with the respective NW releases?

entry.title: Re: How to add Snowflake as a source for SAP Datasphere or SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content: Hi @RajeshG1227 what version of the DP agent are you running? (2.7.6.1 is what you should go for at this time, which is the latest version)

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA direct connectivity with Event Mesh in Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hello Prashant,

   I have done the same steps as you but the channel activation failed in S4HANA 2020 with error @5C\QError@ WebSocket connection set up has failed with error text: 400 Bad Request {} (error type: 1)

  Is the new EMIS , supports only from S4HANA 2021 ?

entry.title: Communication Channel SAP_COM_0179 not available for activation to integrate Profit Center
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Currently, I am implementing SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud for one of our clients.

The client wants to create, change, and delete Profit Centers via API interface. To integrate the Profit Center, we need to activate the communication channel SAP_COM_0179 in the system.

However, during activation, we found that the communication channel SAP_COM_0179 is not available in the system.

I request input from all experts on how to resolve this issue.

entry.title: Re: Not able to create goods issue with BAPI_GOODSMVT_CRE...
entry.content:
Read first

  • FAQ 520813 - FAQ: BAPIs for goods movements 
  • How-To 2420247 - Goods Movement types - SAP ERP & SAP S/4 HANA
  • KBA 2689573 - Available business scenarios and their movement types - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA

for allowed codes and how to use them with this BAPI.

Usually I checked for the required code(s) in those two tables:

  • T158B "Check Table: Movement Type for Transaction Code" to find transactions allowed so correct gm_code to use in BAPI
  • T158G "MMIM: Possible Codes for BAPI Goods Movement" Else BAPI not allowed

entry.title: Re: Distance Metric - Best Matching Technician
entry.content: Thank you, I see the influence request in accepted status. Do you have a timeline of when the feature will be available? - Karthik

entry.title: Spanish User Group (AUSAPE) for Human Capital Management
entry.content:
The Spanish User Group for Human Capital Management (AUSAPE- Grupo de Trabajo de Recursos Humanos y Success Factors) offers regular meetings for customers and partners to support them on HCM topics.

We are inviting all members of the Spanish User Group to take part in the event on the 10th of January 2025!

Agenda:
10:00-10:15. Welcome.
10:15-11:00. Opentext will explain how to maximize the efficiency of Human Resources processes with AI and automation between SAP and OpenText.
11:00-12:30. Gema Moraleda (Localization Product Manager, SAP Globalization, SAP) will talk about the latest legal changes for the new year, annual reports, taxes, and Social Security.

You can find more information and the registration link here.

If you want to connect with SAP users and experts to exchange on HCM topics for Spain, join the Spanish Human Capital Management Localization collaboration group.

entry.title: Re: OData Batch call Operations
entry.content:
Hi Ganesh,
I have an application based on OData v4 Rap based service. I have to perform update as well as create operation in the application. I want to know if the ways specified by you are applicable for OData v4 service as well?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP-CRM V7.13 (non-HANA): Divide data into 2 clients to support a carve out
entry.content: This would be similar to a “Clone and Migrate”-scenario. The idea is to clone client 100 into 200, adjust some customizing afterwards, like integration and connectivity. Then also cloning the orgmodel via SQL and some Business partners, like the org BuPas. These steps are also needed in a ”Clone and Migrate”-scenario. The point is how to get in the masterdata and transactional data most efficiently into the client 200 and I don’t see a reason why not simply changing the client ID for relevant records and running afterwards an update of LOGSYS to the linked ERP-client 200. Any experience or ideas about that?

entry.title: Re: How to send Email Notifications only on the Last Retry Attempt in SAP CI/IS when using AEM
entry.content: Thanks for the detailed blog

entry.title: How Can I Increase performance of Document Parameter Get And Put Request?
entry.content:
Hi, 

I am using sap bo rest api in c# application.

In this app, I am getting document parameters by id using below endpoint;

raylight/v1/documents/{documentId}/parameters

After receiving these parameters, I send a put request to the same endpoint. The payload of the put request includes the parameters from the get request. After making a get, I put again because the values ​​of some parameters are missing after the get request. Due to the size limit, I make a put request after the get to get all the values ​​of the parameters completely.

Sample code is below;

                var documentParametersXml = await _sapClient.GetDocumentParametersByIdAsync(documentId);
                var parsedParameters = ParseDocumentParameters(documentParametersXml);
                var updatePayload = CreateParametersPayload(parsedParameters);
                var updateResponseXml = await _sapCustomPortalClient.UpdateDocumentParametersAsync(documentId, updatePayload);
                return ParseDocumentParameters(updateResponseXml);

So, There is no problem for documents that do not contain many parameters and values, but if the document has many parameters, these get and put requests take a very long time. How can I fix the performance issue here?

entry.title: SAP Build Apps - Delete Item Persistent Storage
entry.content:
Hello

My first post on this forum so excuse me if I have posted to the wrong area...

I have user details saved to local storage called 'persistUser' which is a collection of five records (including user email, password, etc.).

I cannot figure out how to delete it.  I am having trouble navigating this site, so if there are existing solutions, would appreciate being pointed that way.  The only thing I have been able to find is some referencing to looping as I need to delete each record one at a time.

What is really confusing me is that I have the record collection set up with a field called 'key' which is what I am using to identy the record...but when I add logic such as 'Delete record' to the canvas, there is no obvious way I can enter this key so how does the flow know what record to delete?  Really something simple..but I have spend days getting no where...

Any help greatly appreciated.

SAP Build Apps

entry.title: Re: Removing duplicate performance form records from Story query
entry.content: Firstly thank you for pointing out that this is caused by collaborative steps in the form's route map!

entry.title: Re: Existing RFC for Process Director, particularly for accessing data related to the /cockpit/1 t-c
entry.content: Did you look at all RFC function modules by their descriptions? What did you get?

entry.title: Re: Inspection Lot XXXX for order XXXX does not have nece...
entry.content:
Hello @spartan0412 ,

As per your screenshot , inspection lot is having status as ORRQ - it means order record required

Check whether any open point from QM side, just like Open Quality Notifications against Inspection lot or QM stock in inspection.

Regarding ORRQ status, below link is useful to understand this point.

Solved: Process Order- ORRQ - SAP Community

All the best for next steps , ask me further queries🤗
